title: Some results on stochastic differential equations with reflecting boundary conditions
abstract: The authors consider a stochastic differential equation defined on an open connected bounded subset of \(\mathbb{R}\) with reflecting boundary conditions. The main difference to earlier results is that for the drift function only a monotonicity condition of the form \((x-x',b(\omega,t,x)-b(\omega,t,x'))\leq L| x-x'| ^2\) is imposed, substituting the classical Lipschitz condition. The proof relies on the generalization of the Skorokhod problem formulation.

title: Homological spanning forest framework for 2D image analysis
abstract:
The fast growing area of computational (algebraic) topology needs algorithms well-suited to concrete situations. In the topological analysis of a digital picture, a problem is recognizing and taking into account the objects depicted in the image. The main contribution of this paper, in my opinion, is the flexibility of the method with respect of objects of interest.

A Homological Spanning Forest (HSF) plays in homology more or less the role that a spanning tree plays for computing the fundamental group of a cell complex. HSFs have apparently been conceived for homology computation in the case of a 2D image, but their definition is quite general and they are ready for higher dimensional applications.

HSFs are defined as directed subgraphs of the 1-skeleton of a cell complex associated with the pixel structure of a raster image, satisfying a covariance relation between the boundary operator and the discrete vector field determined by the directed edges.

The paper is mainly devoted to introducing and studying the construction and reduction of an HSF out of a 2D raster image; the reduction is necessary for simplifying the homology computation by putting generators in evidence (much in the line of thought of \textit{R. González-Diaz} et al. [Discrete Appl. Math. 157, No. 3, 490--499 (2009; Zbl 1168.68045)]). Moreover, it can be performed while isolating particular objects of the image (Regions Of Interest) and fitting to them.

The article is self-contained, detailed and well supported by meaningful examples. Unfortunately some typos, some less clear passages and the absence of colour in the printed pictures, sometimes make reading a bit difficult.

title: Remarks on the controllability of some stochastic partial differential equations
abstract:
The authors show some approximate and exact-to-zero controllability results in quadratic means for a class of linear stochastic partial differential equations. First the authors consider the case of a stochastic heat equation of the form 
\[
\begin{cases} \partial_t y-\Delta y=1_{\mathcal O} v+B(t) \dot w_t\quad \text{on } D\times (0,T), \\ y=0 \quad\text{on } \partial D \times (0,T), \\ y(0)= y_0\quad \text{in } D, \end{cases}
\]
 where \({\mathcal O}\) and \(D\) are bounded open sets in \(R^N\) with \({\mathcal O} \subset D\), and \(\dot w_t\) is a Gaussian white noise in time. It is proved that the set of terminal variables \(y_v(T)\), when the control \(v\) belongs to the space of \(L^2(D)\)-valued square integrable and adapted processes, is a dense subspace of \(L^2 (\Omega; L^2(D))\). Moreover, if the coefficient \(B\) is nonrandom and satisfies a suitable technical condition, then one can also choose \(v\) in such a way that \(y_v(T) =0\). Similar questions are studied for Stokes and quasi-Stokes stochastic systems.

For the entire collection see [Zbl 0889.00041].
venue: Desch, W. (ed.) et al., Control and estimation of distributed parameter systems. International conference in Vorau, Austria, July 14--20, 1996. Basel: Birkhäuser. ISNM, Int. Ser. Numer. Math. 126, 141-151 (1998).

title: Stable cones in the thin one-phase problem
abstract:
The paper studies the fractional Bernoulli one-phase problem (falling in the class of free boundary problems) associated with the critical points and minimizers of the energy functional
\[
\mathcal{J}_{\Lambda}(u)=\frac{c_{n,s}}{2}\int_{\mathbb{R}^n}\int_{\mathbb{R}^n} \frac{(u(x)-u(y))^2}{|x-y|^{n+2s}}\,dx\,dy+\Lambda^2|\{u>0\}|, \quad s\in(0,1),\ \Lambda\in\mathbb{R}.
\]
The main result states that global stable critical points (in particular, minimizers) of this energy, which are also \(s\)-homogeneous and axially symmetric, are necessarily one-dimensional if the dimension \(n\leq 5\).

The authors conjecture that, actually, all global, stable, \(s\)-homogeneous solutions are one-dimensional up to \(n\leq 6\).

The carried out analysis relies on a characterization of the stability condition which, surprisingly, makes use of the notion of ``large solutions'' for the fractional Laplacian, i.e., a class of solutions with prescribed singularity along the boundary of the underlying domain.

title: Existence and uniqueness of solutions for delay stochastic evolution equations of second order in time
abstract:
The authors consider stochastic delay evolution equations of second order in time of the form 
\[
\begin{multlined} u'(t) + \int_0^t A(s)u(s) \,ds + \int_0^t B(s,u'(s)) \,ds = \psi'(0) + \int_0^t (F_1(s,u_s,u'_s) + F_2(s,u_s,u'_s)+ f(s))\,ds\\ +\int_0^t (G_0(s,u_s,u'_s) + G_1(s,u_s,u'_s)+ g(s))\,dW(s), \end{multlined}
\]
 for \(t\in [0,T]\) and \(u(t)=\psi(t)\) for \(t \in [-h,0]\). Here \(W\) is a real-valued Wiener process and \(A\), \(B\), etc., are appropriate operators. A special case is given by 
\[
\frac{\partial^2 u}{\partial t^2}-\frac{\partial^2 u}{\partial x^2}-\beta \frac{\partial^2 u}{\partial x^2} \left(\frac{\partial u}{\partial t}\right)= g\left(t,u(t-\sigma_1(t)),\frac{\partial u}{\partial x}(t-\sigma_2(t))\right)\frac{\partial W(t)}{\partial t}
\]
 in \([0,T]\times[0,1]\), where \(u(t)=\psi(t)\) for \(t \in [-h,0]\) and \(u(t,0)=u(t,1)=0\) for \(t\in [0,T]\). This problem can be viewed as a model of the longitudinal displacement \(u\) of a strongly damped stretched string of unit length whose ends are fixed, where one assumes that the string is subject to some random external force that depends on the past of the state.

The authors prove existence and uniqueness of solutions to the general problem. Results of \textit{E. Pardoux} [``Équations aux dérivées partielles stochastiques non linéaires monotones'' (Thèse, Université Paris XI, 1975)], iteration techniques and Galerkin methods are ingredients of the proof. The discussion of two examples, a Neumann problem for a damped string and a problem for a strongly damped wave equation, conclude the article.

abstract: It is proved that in the case of a stochastic Stokes system the approximate controllability is equivalent to the unique continuation property for a backward stochastic system.

title: Pullback attractors for non-autonomous reaction-diffusion equations with dynamical boundary conditions
abstract: The existence of pullback attractors for non autonomous reaction diffusion equations subject to dynamical boundary conditions is proved. A weak formulation of the problem is provided and the existence and uniqueness of weak solutions is proved using a monotonicity method based on energy estimates. This method allows the generalization of some rather restrictive conditions on the data of the problem that were used in the existing literature. Then a continuous dependence result on the initial data of the problem is proved which is needed for establishing the asymptotic compactness results, required for the study of the asymptotic behaviour of the flow. Finally, the existence of pullback attractors in various settings is proved and the relation between them is commented upon. The equation studied finds interesting applications in hydrodynamics and heat transfer.

title: On the stochastic 3D-Lagrangian averaged Navier-Stokes \(\alpha\)-model with finite delay
abstract: The authors study existence and uniqueness of solutions to a stochastic version of the 3D-Lagrangian averaged Navier-Stokes equation in a bounded domain.

title: The exponential behaviour of nonlinear stochastic functional equations of second order in time
abstract:
The authors consider stochastic delay evolution equations of second order in time of the form 
\[
u'(t) = v(t), \quad t\in [0,T],\tag{1}
\]
 and 
\[
v(t) = \int_0^t A(s)u(s) ds + \int_0^t B(s,v(s)) ds = v_0 + \int_0^t F(s,u_s,v_s) ds +\int_0^t G(s,u_s,v_s) dW(s)
\]
 for \(t\geq 0\) and \(u(0) = u_0\), \(u(t)=\varphi_1(t)\), \(v(t)= \varphi_2(t)\) for \(t \in (-h,0)\). Here \(W\) is a real-valued Wiener process and \(A\), \(B\), etc. are appropriate operators. A special case is given by 
\[
\frac{\partial^2 u}{\partial t^2}-a^2\frac{\partial^2 u}{\partial x^2}+\theta \frac{\partial u}{\partial t} =\sigma \frac{\partial u(t-\tau(t))}{\partial x}\frac{d W(t)}{d t}\tag{2}
\]
 in \((0,+\infty)\times(0,1)\), where \(u(t,0)=u(t,1)=0\) for \(t\in (0,+\infty)\), \(u(0,x)=u_0\), \( \frac{\partial u}{\partial t}(0,x)= v_0(x)\) in \((0,1)\) and \(u(t)=\varphi_1(t)\), \( \frac{\partial u}{\partial t}= \varphi_2(t)\) for \(t \in [-h,0]\). This problem can be viewed as a model of the lateral displacement \(u\) of a vibrating string where one assumes that the string is subject to some random external force that depends on the past of the state. The authors investigate the exponential stability of the zero solution of system (1). In particular they consider exponential stability in mean-square, such that for the solution \((u,v)\) of (1) one has 
\[
E(| v(t)| ^2 + \| u(t)\| ^2) \leq K_1 (E| v_0| ^2 + E \| u_0\| ^2 + | \varphi_2| ^2_{I_H^2} + \| \varphi_1\| ^2_{I_V^2}) e^{-mt}\tag{3}
\]
 and they provide sufficient conditions for (3) to hold. The proof starts with applying the Itô formula to the process \(e^{mt}| v(t)| ^2 + e^{mt}\langle A(t)u(t),u(t)\rangle\) and then proceeds with estimating the resulting terms. Further they conclude pathwise exponential stability from exponential stability in mean-square via a technique employing the Burkholder-Davis-Gundy and the Doob inequality and the Borel-Cantelli lemma. The discussion of several examples, among them (2), concludes the article.

title: Einstein-Kähler metrics on manifolds with positive first Chern class
abstract: Let \(M\) be a compact Kähler manifold. The existence of a Kähler- Einstein metric is closely related to the sign of the first Chern class \(C_ 1\) of \(M\). For \(C_ 1<0\) (resp. \(C_ 1=0\)), \textit{T. Aubin} in C. R. Acad. Sci., Paris Sér. A 283, 119-121 (1976; Zbl 0333.53040); resp. \textit{S. T. Yau} in Commun. Pure Appl. Math. 31, 339-411 (1978; Zbl 0362.53049)] produced an affirmative answer. Moreover, to study the case \(C_ 1>0\), \textit{T. Aubin} introduced the invariant \(\xi\) [J. Funct. Anal. Appl. 57, 143-153 (1984; Zbl 0538.53063)]. On the other hand, also for this last case, \textit{G. Tian} [Invent. Math. 89, 225-246 (1987; Zbl 0599.53046)] introduced the invariants \(\alpha\) (similar to \(\xi\)) and \(\alpha_ G(M)\). The second one depends upon the symmetries of \(M\) and was till now calculated for complex dimension two and for some particular cases of Fermat surfaces. The present author shows that \(\alpha(\mathbb{P}_ m\mathbb{C})=1/(m+1)\) (a result already announced, but not proved by Tian) and \(\alpha_ G(\mathbb{P}_ m\mathbb{C})=(m+1)\alpha(\mathbb{P}_ m\mathbb{C})\). As a consequence, the blowing up of \(\mathbb{P}_ m\mathbb{C}\) in \((m+1)\) points can bear a Kähler-Einstein metric.

title: Some results on controllability for stochastic heat and Stokes equations
abstract: Summary: This note is concerned with some results on approximate controllability for a stochastic heat equation and a stochastic system of the Stokes kind. These are extensions to the stochastic situation of well-known \textit{J.-L. Lions} results in the deterministic case [in: Control of distributed systems. Grupo Anál. Mat. Apl. Univ. Málaga 3, 77-87 (1990; Zbl 0752.93037)].

title: Intuitionistic/constructive accounts of the continuum today
abstract:
The chapter provides a nice overview of constructive real analysis (a branch of constructive mathematics) and smooth infinitesimal analysis, two alternative approaches to real analysis based on constructive real numbers and microquantities, respectively. Both approaches require the use of intuitionistic (constructive) logic. The exposition is accessible to non-experts, and the introductory section contains some historical remarks on Brouwer's view of the continuum.

For the entire collection see [Zbl 1454.01002].
venue: Shapiro, Stewart (ed.) et al., The history of continua. Philosophical and mathematical perspectives. Oxford: Oxford University Press. 476-501 (2021).

title: zbMATH Open Web Interface contents unavailable due to conflicting licenses.
abstract: The authors develop various calculus rules for Fréchet lower and upper subgradients of extended-real-valued functions on real Banach spaces. Optimality conditions for large classes of nonsmooth constrained optimization problems are then derived.
